focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Tiffany works as a psychic, taking a call from Margaret while Ted mocks her job; they bicker over their relationship and the nature of her work, with tension mixed with playful moments.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Distressed caller to psychic hotline
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Expresses anxiety and anger about a strained relationship with someone she cares about
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Relationship frustration remains unaddressed
evidence_quotes: Margaret, I'm getting the feeling this is an anxious day for you.
You've got to learn to channel that rage, Margaret.
I feel someone you really care about, maybe even the love of your life, is being a real pain.

relation_updates: Relationship with Paul implied strained
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Anxious and angry individual seeking external help

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul watches a home video of Margaret laughing off a spill, then finds Raymond's birth certificate in a box, using it to fill out a credit card application under the name Raymond Barnell.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Paul's wife reacting to news of Raymond's return
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Hears Paul say Raymond is back and rushes out of the kitchen.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Absent from the kitchen after abruptly leaving.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Why she has a strong negative reaction to Raymond's return.
evidence_quotes: There's actually something we need to talk about. It's about Raymond. He's back.
She rushes from the room.

relation_updates: Tension with Paul over Raymond's return
status_updates: Upset and absent from the kitchen

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul and Margaret discuss Raymond's absence with relief; Paul mentions he may return south soon, then the phone rings as they interact nervously.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Margaret as Paul's wife, in the kitchen discussing Raymond's absence
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Expresses nervousness about Raymond not being present and smiles relieved when Paul says Raymond may head back South soon
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Relieved after hearing Raymond's potential departure from Paul
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Raymond will actually leave as mentioned
evidence_quotes: MARGARET (nervous) He's not here, is he?
MARGARET When he's around, it just brings back... Fuck.
Margaret smiles, relieved.

relation_updates: Negative relationship with Raymond reinforced
Positive, reassuring interaction with Paul
status_updates: Emotional state shifts from nervous to relieved
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Nervous wife anxious about Raymond's presence, reassured by Paul

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted, stressed over a career-defining case, clashes with Tiffany about their life and her view of service workers; she leaves upset as he retreats into a film, ignoring her.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Wife emotionally overwhelmed by HMO denial letter, supported by husband
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Breaks down crying over the HMO letter and is comforted by Paul on the sofa
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Paul cradles Margaret on the sofa, reassuring her and promising to find specialists, but her condition remains undiagnosed and the HMO denial stands
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: HMO denial of medical claims and Margaret's undiagnosed illness
evidence_quotes: Margaret starts to CRY, holding the letter up to her face the SOBS building to a full blown breakdown.
He takes her to a small sofa, cradles her in his arms.
MARGARET I'm sick! I'm really, really sick!
PAUL I know, Sweetie. I know. And we're gonna find you the best specialists in... (a second, then confident) ...what you have that we can.

relation_updates: Paul comforts Margaret and promises to find specialists
Margaret expresses anger towards HMO
status_updates: Threatens self-harm in response to denial

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Gary grabs his gun but is hit by a vacuum, causing nosebleed; Margaret calls the police, throws the phone at him, and he aims his gun at her head before ducking, realizing the danger.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Defends herself against intruder Gary in the garage
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Throws objects at Gary and attempts to call police, leading to him aiming a gun at her
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To protect herself and seek help
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Gary aims his gun at her head
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Immediate threat from armed intruder
evidence_quotes: A Bissel Handvac catches him flush in the face.
Margaret reaches for her portable phone and starts punching numbers.
Gary stands, pissed off, gun aimed at Margaret's head.

relation_updates: Adversarial conflict with Gary escalates to physical violence
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Fearful but aggressive in self-defense

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Margaret, tied to a chair, insists her Tourette's is real amid Gary's skepticism; he mocks her, threatens her with a gun, and grows tense as she defiantly challenges his authority.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Defiant hostage
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Margaret insists her Tourette's is real, curses at Gary, and threatens to yell, causing him to become confused and embarrassed.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To have Gary acknowledge her Tourette's diagnosis as legitimate and stop mocking her.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Gary is confused and embarrassed, holding his gun but unable to control Margaret or respond effectively.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Gary will retaliate with his gun after her defiance.
evidence_quotes: It's a disease. It's called Tourette Syndrome. I can't control what I say. Turnip.
You've taken a handicapped person hostage. I hope you're proud of yourself.
Ass-rimming ball-sucker.
Gary, confused and embarrassed, doesn't know what to do.

relation_updates: Escalates hostile dynamic with Gary through direct defiance
status_updates: Shifts from passive captive to active resistor
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Defiant and unafraid captive who uses her condition to challenge authority.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted hears the doorknob, notices the computer, and tries to reclaim the headset from Tiffany, who resists while talking to Margaret.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Margaret attempting to report an armed intruder to Tiffany via Ted
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Margaret tells Ted there is a man with a gun in her shower, expressing immediate danger
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Seek help for the armed intruder
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Crisis communicated to Ted, Tiffany assumes control of the call
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Armed intruder in the shower
evidence_quotes: MARGARET Oh thank fucking Christ. Is Tiffany there?
MARGARET Well you should know, fuck-wit. You know there's a man with a gun in my shower, right now?

relation_updates: Reaffirms reliance on Tiffany for crisis support
status_updates: In immediate danger from armed intruder

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted arrives in the lot; Paul, Jimbo, and Gary exit their cars. Paul checks on Margaret in Jimbo's car, confirming she's present and telling them to let her go.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Captive whose release is negotiated by her husband
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul arrives and instructs Jimbo to release Margaret after confirming she is in the car.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Margaret remains lying in the backseat of Jimbo's car, not yet released.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Her physical release and reunion with Paul.
evidence_quotes: Paul looks for Margaret in the back of Jimbo's car
but she remains lying down on the backseat
PAUL Yes. You can let her go now.

relation_updates: Paul confirms the exchange and orders her release
status_updates: Release from captivity is now authorized

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Margaret survives, winded but unharmed, with the bullet stopped by a money stack; Paul weeps in relief. Gary, shaken after shooting Raymond, is comforted by Jimbo. Both couples reunite and leave together.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Survivor of a shooting
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Survives being shot in the back when a stack of money stops the bullet
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Alive, unharmed but winded, and leaving with Paul
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Aftermath of the violent confrontation
evidence_quotes: He moves the barrel six inches to the right and shoots Margaret in the back.
She drops face first into the snow.
Margaret, alive, unharmed but winded, tries to catch her breath.

relation_updates: Paul demonstrates care and relief after her survival
status_updates: From shot and down to alive and unharmed but winded

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul reads a nostalgic postcard from Margaret, who appears ready to leave; they share a warm moment, pack keepsakes, and depart together as a cab waits outside.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Departing home with Paul
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Margaret asks if Paul is ready, picks up Jodie's carrying case, and responds positively to Paul's question about her feelings before they leave together.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Left the house with Paul to catch a waiting cab
evidence_quotes: MARGARET (O.S.) (cont'd) Paul, you ready?
Margaret picks up Jodie, whose in his carrying case, and walks over.
A CAR HORN sounds from outside. It's their cab.
MARGARET Pretty damn good.

relation_updates: Affirms positive state to Paul
Shares affectionate hand squeeze with Paul

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Margaret Barnell begins as a distressed wife seeking psychic advice for relationship issues with Paul. She reacts anxiously to her brother-in-law Raymond's return and suffers a health crisis, leading to a breakdown. During a home invasion, she becomes a defiant captive, disclosing her Tourette's and seeking help. After being freed by Paul, she survives a shooting and they reconcile, departing together.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 12、INT. INSURANCE OFFICE / BRANCH'S OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul Barnell seeks to cash in his brother Raymond's life insurance after five years of disappearance, but Ted Watters explains Alaska law requires seven years before legal death declaration, making payout impossible now.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Claimant attempting to cash missing brother's life insurance policy
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul is denied the insurance payout because Ted explains that under Alaskan law, a person must be missing for seven years before being declared legally dead, and Raymond has only been gone for five years.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To receive the life insurance payout for Raymond's policy.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Paul's claim is rejected, and he must wait at least two more years for legal proceedings.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The insurance claim cannot be processed until Raymond has been missing for seven years.
evidence_quotes: he's been gone for five years now, and I thought it might be time to... move on.
cash in Raymond's policy.
under Alaskan statutes a person must be missing for seven years before he or she can be legally declared dead

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Mild-mannered man in a cheap suit, hinting at financial strain while seeking insurance benefits.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul searches the trash for his phone, finding it on the mouth of a corpse in a suit, while the caller still speaks, unaware.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Discoverer of a corpse
timeline_nodes.salient_development: While searching for his lost phone in the trash, Paul finds it lying across the mouth of a dead body in a dumpster.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Shocked by the discovery of a corpse
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: identity and circumstances of the corpse's death
evidence_quotes: Paul, muttering, leans in to get it.
He pushes aside an orange garbage bag, guided by the faint sound of the other person STILL TALKING.

status_updates: now aware of a dead body in his vicinity
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Person who accidentally discovered a body while searching for his phone

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul hides a body in the fridge, packs it with drinks, sets the coldest temperature, locks it with a chain, hides evidence, and reenters the house casually asking about mail.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Concealer of a corpse in garage fridge
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul forces the dead man's body into the garage fridge, snaps the knee to fit it, packs with drinks, sets coldest temperature, locks the fridge with a chain, and hides evidence before entering the house.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to hide the body and cover up his involvement
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Body secured in locked fridge; Paul enters house and casually asks about mail.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether the body will be discovered despite concealment.
evidence_quotes: He SHOOS the dog back into the house.
with great effort picks up the dead man and tries to stuff him inside.
He grits his teeth, looks away as he kicks at the knee. It SNAPS sideways.
A padlock and chain come from the base of a propane BBQ. It goes around the fridge, the lock SNAPS it in place.

relation_updates: interacts with wife Margaret while concealing actions
status_updates: has concealed a corpse in his garage fridge
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Determined concealer of evidence, acting to avoid detection.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul watches a home video of Margaret laughing off a spill, then finds Raymond's birth certificate in a box, using it to fill out a credit card application under the name Raymond Barnell.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Identity fraudster
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul finds Raymond's birth certificate in a document box and begins filling out a credit card application under Raymond's name.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To obtain credit through fraudulent means.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Paul has started a credit card application using Raymond Barnell's identity.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether the fraud will succeed and its consequences.
evidence_quotes: Paul takes out the credit card application from work, and starts to write... PAUL (to himself) Raymond... Barnell.

relation_updates: Exploits brother Raymond's identity for personal gain
status_updates: Moral status shifts as he engages in criminal activity
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Individual committing identity fraud with family documents

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul is interviewed by reporters while Ted finds a photo of Raymond; Gary and Jimbo continue arguing over exhuming a body, with Jimbo expressing regret.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Paul uses the funeral to set up a media interview for insurance pressure.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul tells Ted he called TV reporters to the funeral, planning to use media coverage to pressure the insurance company into paying Raymond's policy.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Force insurance payment by leveraging public media attention.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Paul is ready to give a media interview with photos of Raymond, aiming to shame the insurance company.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether media pressure will successfully compel the insurance company to pay.
evidence_quotes: I called them.
Well, they've been asking me to talk some more about Raymond. I thought now might be an appropriate time.

relation_updates: Paul openly confronts Ted about insurance company's failure to pay
Paul manages Margaret by sending her to car to avoid public embarrassment
status_updates: Paul transitions from passive mourner to active media strategist
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Paul acts as an opportunist manipulating the funeral setting for financial gain.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Gary threatens to kill Paul's wife unless he delivers the body and $50,000 in three days, warning of consequences if police are involved or demands aren't met.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Paul is threatened by Gary with an ultimatum to deliver the body and money or his wife will be killed.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Gary exposes Paul's insurance scam and demands he retrieve the body and pay $50,000 within three days, threatening to kill his wife if he fails or involves police.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Deliver the body and $50,000 within three days to prevent his wife's death.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Paul must comply with Gary's demands to protect his wife from immediate harm.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Paul can meet the demands and deadline to save his wife.
evidence_quotes: Here's how it's going to work. We stay here with your wife, you go get our guy.
We even see you or a cop even roll past, she's dead.
Have the body and, let's say 50,000 dollars for our trouble.

relation_updates: Gary becomes primary antagonist with explicit threats
status_updates: Paul is now under a deadline with his wife's life at stake
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Husband coerced into criminal actions to safeguard his family.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Raymond spares Paul, learns he faked his death for life insurance; they discuss the payout, split, and Paul's failing relationship with Margaret, while Raymond reveals he gave up carbs.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Confronted by returned brother Raymond
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Raymond assaults Paul, references a newspaper article about his supposed death, and demands half of the $50,000 life insurance payout, which Paul agrees to.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To survive the assault and agree to Raymond's demand for half the insurance money.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Beaten and wheezing, sitting on the floor after agreeing to split the insurance money with Raymond.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to split the money and manage Raymond's return.
evidence_quotes: PAUL Life insurance.

relation_updates: Raymond returns and violently confronts Paul
admits marriage to Margaret is failing
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Paul, who faked Raymond's death for insurance money, is now forced to share it.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted challenges Paul's claim by noting a missing birthmark on the body, suggesting fraud; Paul accepts a settlement to avoid exhumation and dental verification, while Ted's junior gets promoted for solving a robbery case.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Paul accepts a settlement after Ted threatens to verify the body through exhumation and dental records
timeline_nodes.salient_development: When Ted proposes digging up the body and checking dental records, Paul goes white and accepts the settlement Branch offers to end the investigation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Paul receives agreement for a settlement check to be delivered the next morning, avoiding further verification of the body's identity.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The body is not Raymond's, and dental verification would expose the fraud.
evidence_quotes: Dental records! Paul goes white. He hadn't thought about this.
We'll have a check brought over to you tomorrow morning.
Paul nods, stressed and strung out, he hugs the wall as he walks out.

relation_updates: Conflict with Ted Watters resolved by settlement acceptance
status_updates: Insurance claim settled to avoid further investigation
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A man who accepts a settlement to prevent exhumation and dental record comparison

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 94、INT. BARNELL MOBILE HOME / BACK BEDROOM - MOMENTS LATER
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Raymond suggests ending Margaret's influence, comparing her to a sick dog that must be put down; Paul, heartbroken, seems to agree, viewing her as untrustworthy and destructive.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Paul confides in Raymond about Margaret's infidelity and hears Raymond's advice.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul tells Raymond about Margaret's affairs with her therapist and others, and after Raymond compares her to a sick dog that needs putting down, Paul nods in agreement.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Paul is heartbroken and has nodded in agreement with Raymond's suggestion to take action against Margaret.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Paul will act on Raymond's suggestion to harm Margaret.
evidence_quotes: PAUL I went by there. She was in the bedroom with the big one. (quietly) Not the first time, either.
RAYMOND Nothing you can do but put the thing out of it's misery. And yours.
As Ray stands to go, Paul nods, his heart ripped apart for any number of reasons.

relation_updates: Paul's trust in Margaret is destroyed
Paul aligns with Raymond against Margaret
status_updates: Paul now views Margaret as a destructive threat
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A husband betrayed by his wife's infidelity, now considering drastic measures.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Margaret survives, winded but unharmed, with the bullet stopped by a money stack; Paul weeps in relief. Gary, shaken after shooting Raymond, is comforted by Jimbo. Both couples reunite and leave together.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Paul witnesses Margaret being shot by Raymond and rushes to her aid.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Margaret is shot in the back by Raymond but survives when a stack of money stops the bullet; Paul weeps in relief after finding her alive; Gary shoots Raymond, who falls wounded; both couples leave the parking area together.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To reach and help Margaret after she is shot.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Margaret is alive but winded; Raymond is incapacitated by a gunshot wound; Paul and Margaret reunite with Jimbo and Gary, and all leave together.
evidence_quotes: Paul's eyes shut tight, expecting the worst.
Paul, almost crazed with grief, cradles his wife's body.
Margaret, alive, unharmed but winded, tries to catch her breath.

relation_updates: Paul and Raymond are revealed to be brothers.
Paul's bond with Margaret is reinforced by the traumatic event.
status_updates: A stack of money stops the bullet.
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Paul is protective and emotionally overwhelmed but relieved after Margaret's survival.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul reads a nostalgic postcard from Margaret, who appears ready to leave; they share a warm moment, pack keepsakes, and depart together as a cab waits outside.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Paul leaves his home with Margaret after she agrees to his question.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Paul reads Margaret's postcard where she says 'yes' to his question, and they depart together.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Paul and Margaret are in a cab leaving their home.
evidence_quotes: Paul, sitting alone, reads a postcard from Margaret.
MARGARET (O.S.) (cont'd) Paul, you ready?
A CAR HORN sounds from outside. It's their cab.
Paul takes her hand and gives it a squeeze. They head out.

relation_updates: Margaret says 'yes' to Paul's question, and they leave together
status_updates: Starting a new life with Margaret
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Paul is departing with Margaret, having received her affirmative response.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Paul Barnell, financially strained after his brother Raymond's disappearance, discovers a corpse in a dumpster and conceals it instead of reporting it. He commits insurance fraud using Raymond's identity, leading to threats from criminals and investigator Ted Watters. After Raymond returns alive and is killed in a confrontation, Paul and his wife Margaret survive and leave together to start anew.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Howard struggles to unload a heavy snowmobile with Ted, who secretly records him; Howard notices the camera as they chat, revealing the scene is footage on a VCR.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: secret recorder
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Offers help to Howard while secretly recording him with a camcorder.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Howard has noticed Ted recording him and questions his actions.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Howard's reaction to being recorded.
evidence_quotes: who has taken out a small cam-corder and is taping Howard.

relation_updates: Ted meets Howard and secretly records him
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Records Howard without his knowledge or consent.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 12、INT. INSURANCE OFFICE / BRANCH'S OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul Barnell seeks to cash in his brother Raymond's life insurance after five years of disappearance, but Ted Watters explains Alaska law requires seven years before legal death declaration, making payout impossible now.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: by-the-book insurance agent
timeline_nodes.salient_development: denies Paul Barnell's insurance claim by explaining Alaska's seven-year missing person rule
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ted has denied the claim and closed the file based on legal requirements
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Paul's reaction to the denial and Branch's dissatisfaction with Ted's demeanor
evidence_quotes: With no actual body, under Alaskan statutes a person must be missing for seven years before he or she can be legally declared dead
Ted flips the file shut. Case closed.

relation_updates: Ted meets Paul Barnell and denies his insurance claim
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Ted enforces legal rules strictly, prioritizing procedure over personal circumstances

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Branch asserts control over corporate narrative, dismisses concerns, and ends the operation, with Cam complying to avoid further conflict.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Investigator arguing to continue Barnell case
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Branch dismisses Barnell case due to PR concerns and removes Ted from it
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Ted seeks to continue official investigation but is overruled
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Barnell case closed by Branch, Ted removed from case
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Ted's suspicion about the Barnell case remains unaddressed
evidence_quotes: TED This isn't a standard case. You were there when this guy came in looking to cash in. C'mon, it stinks.
Cam, seeing his partner is about to get himself into a heap of trouble, hustles him toward the door.

relation_updates: Branch asserts authority over Ted
Cam intervenes to protect Ted
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Ted persists in advocating for the case despite opposition

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted confides in Cam that something is wrong after visiting someone, urging Cam to help delay the credit checks; Cam agrees reluctantly in exchange for more transparency and better communication from Ted.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: investigator secretly pursuing case with partner's help
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted convinces Cam to delay credit checks on the Barnells by promising future transparency and involvement.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Delay credit checks for one week to continue the investigation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Cam agrees to help delay the credit checks, establishing a secret partnership.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Risk of discovery and trouble for unauthorized actions.
evidence_quotes: Help me buy a week.
I promise from now on you can come along on everything.
Good. Now if you'll excuse me, I'll go start covering your ass.

relation_updates: Cam becomes active accomplice in secret investigation
Ted promises greater transparency to Cam
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Forms a secret alliance with Cam to bypass official procedures.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted, stressed over a career-defining case, clashes with Tiffany about their life and her view of service workers; she leaves upset as he retreats into a film, ignoring her.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: stressed insurance investigator arguing with girlfriend Tiffany
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted argues with Tiffany about his career stress and their relationship, leading her to storm off
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ted is alone on the couch watching a film, ignoring the conflict
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: argument with Tiffany is unresolved as she leaves and he retreats
evidence_quotes: TIFFANY Why you gotta argue with the service industry?
Tiffany storms off as Ted flops onto the couch, swipes the snow from the top of the pizza box and hits play on the DVD.
Ted doesn't move, lost in his own world.

relation_updates: Tiffany storms off after argument
Relationship strain due to Ted's career stress
status_updates: Ted is stressed over a career-defining case
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Ted's career obsession strains his personal relationship

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Tiffany reviews Ted's case files, finds the dead man had a violent past, and urges Ted to re-examine the information, hinting at new implications.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: ignoring partner's concerns while obsessing over case footage
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted replays body footage repeatedly; Tiffany grabs the remote in frustration and points out the dead man's violent record in the files.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ted remains engrossed in the footage, but Tiffany has identified a potential lead in the dead man's past.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The significance of the dead man's violent past for the case.
evidence_quotes: Ted on the floor, remote control in hand, replaying the shots of the body over and over...
Shhhh. This is the important part.
Ted rewinds the tape, hits play, rewinds it again
Finally, she walks over and grabs the remote control.

relation_updates: Strain with Tiffany due to case obsession
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Ted prioritizes case analysis over Tiffany's immediate concerns.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul, injured and bleeding, is confronted by Ted, who leaves after Mrs. Wherry intervenes with a bow; she offers to stitch Paul's wound, sparking a thought.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Accuser confronting Paul Barnell
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted accuses Paul of murder and threatens jail, leading to Paul falling during confrontation and Mrs. Wherry intervening with a bow.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To have Paul jailed for murder
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ted leaves the driveway after Mrs. Wherry aims a compound bow at him
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Paul's guilt and the truth about the murder accusation
evidence_quotes: Seeing as how you killed that man and dumped his body out in the woods.
You are going to jail. I will personally see to that.
You are a liar and thief.

relation_updates: Hostile confrontation with Paul Barnell
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Escalates with direct murder accusations and personal threats

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted challenges Paul's claim by noting a missing birthmark on the body, suggesting fraud; Paul accepts a settlement to avoid exhumation and dental verification, while Ted's junior gets promoted for solving a robbery case.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Insurance investigator whose fraud accusation is dismissed by his boss.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted presents evidence of a missing birthmark to prove fraud, but Paul claims laser surgery; Branch orders Ted to deliver an apology check and promotes Cam.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To prove Paul's death claim fraudulent by showing the body lacks a known birthmark.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Branch orders Ted to deliver an apology check to Paul and announces Cam's promotion for solving a robbery case.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Ted remains convinced of Paul's guilt but is overruled by Branch and unable to pursue further action.

relation_updates: Loss of standing with boss Branch
Cam promoted over Ted
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Frustrated and demoralized after his investigation is shut down and his junior is promoted.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Raymond attacks Ted at his car, threatening him and demanding money before taking his wallet and leaving.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: victim of violent assault
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Hit in the face by Raymond, head squeezed in car door, and wallet stolen.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Injured and without wallet after assault.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Raymond's threat and demand for money.
evidence_quotes: A fist to his face drops Ted.
lays Ted's head on the door jamb.
Listen you little prick, we've had just about enough of this. You get me?
Ted SCREAMING as his head is squashed in the vice.

relation_updates: Adversarial relationship with Raymond escalates to physical violence
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Physical harm inflicted by Raymond.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Tiffany, on a psychic hotline, reconnects with Ted who sincerely asks her to join him; touched, she agrees, ending the call to support him.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Ted asks Tiffany to join him in finding Margaret, and she agrees to help.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted returns and sincerely invites Tiffany to accompany him; she accepts, ending her call to support him.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to locate Margaret with Tiffany's assistance.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ted and Tiffany depart together to find Margaret.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the search for Margaret is ongoing.
evidence_quotes: TED: OK. Come on.

relation_updates: Relationship with Tiffany repaired and strengthened
Tiffany becomes active ally
status_updates: Has Tiffany's support for the mission
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Ted acknowledges his neglect and seeks Tiffany's partnership.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ted attacks Paul in a chaotic fight fueled by emotional strain; Margaret and Tiffany intervene to separate them as tensions over love and humiliation peak.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Aggressor in a physical confrontation with Paul
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Physically attacks Paul after demanding answers, escalating from verbal confrontation to a chaotic fight.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To force answers from Paul about his humiliating treatment.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Engaged in a fight that requires Margaret and Tiffany to intervene and separate them.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Unresolved anger and humiliation driving his violent behavior.
evidence_quotes: I have been beaten, humiliated, had my head squeezed by a guy... and I am getting some answers, right now.
Ted, barely able to stay on his feet (concussion) gets in front of Paul, and just stands there and waits.
The punch hits Paul on the top of his head.
Margaret and Tiffany jump in, trying to pull the two men apart.

relation_updates: Hostile physical confrontation with Paul
Tiffany and Margaret intervene
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Delirious, damaged, and concussed, acting on volatile emotions.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Paul reads a nostalgic postcard from Margaret, who appears ready to leave; they share a warm moment, pack keepsakes, and depart together as a cab waits outside.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Ted addresses ethical concerns about the life insurance payout and shares a romantic moment with Tiffany
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ted explains his ethical stance on the life insurance policy and holds hands with Tiffany as they walk in the snow
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to proceed to his rescheduled appointment and spend time with Tiffany
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: ethically reassured and romantically connected with Tiffany
evidence_quotes: TIFFANY Good. I like it when you're okay.
Tiffany grabs his hand and pulls him away, disappearing into the whiteness.

relation_updates: Romantic intimacy with Tiffany
Ethical reconciliation with his actions
status_updates: Emotionally settled about the insurance case
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Shows acceptance of his ethical reasoning and affectionate behavior towards Tiffany

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Ted Watters starts as a disengaged insurance agent who secretly records colleagues and denies claims by the book. His obsession with the Barnell case leads to professional setbacks, unauthorized investigations, and personal deterioration, including conflict with his girlfriend Tiffany. After physical assaults and emotional volatility, he repairs his relationship with Tiffany and resolves his ethical concerns, finding peace.
